open_findings.title: Potential null dereference in conversation deletion without soft-delete

history.notes: Status synced from Linear (PLP-96): Todo -> accepted
Status synced from Linear (PLP-96): In Review -> fixed_pending_verify
Status synced from Linear (PLP-96): In Progress -> in_progress
Status synced from Linear (PLP-96): In Review -> fixed_pending_verify
Verified by source review: deleteConversation uses soft-delete and guards missing/deleted conversations before participant access.
Status changed by synthesizer synthesized-20260427-223921

open_findings.description: Conversation deletion now checks that the conversation exists and is not already soft-deleted before accessing participant fields, then soft-deletes by setting deletedAt and maps Prisma P2025 races to NotFoundException.
open_findings.impact: The delete path no longer dereferences a missing conversation and no longer hard-deletes the row; concurrent delete races return a controlled not-found response.

proof_hooks.summary: deleteConversation fetches the conversation, throws NotFoundException when missing/deleted, validates participant membership, and updates deletedAt instead of deleting the row.
The update is wrapped in a try/catch that converts Prisma P2025 into NotFoundException for concurrent missing/already-deleted rows.

suggested_fix.approach: Review the existing case file and apply the documented remediation.
tests_needed: Re-run the relevant LYRA audit agent.

open_findings.title: Unsafe profile access in follows.service.ts without existence check

history.notes: Status synced from Linear (PLP-97): Todo -> accepted
Status synced from Linear (PLP-97): In Review -> fixed_pending_verify
Status synced from Linear (PLP-97): In Progress -> in_progress
Status synced from Linear (PLP-97): In Review -> fixed_pending_verify
Fix remains incomplete: profile update path can still fail and now duplicates counter increments.
Verification failed: profile updates are still unsafe for missing Profile rows and now duplicate counter increments.
Status changed by synthesizer synthesized-20260427-223921

open_findings.description: The target user existence check exists, but followUser still calls tx.profile.update for both users inside the transaction. If either Profile row is absent, Prisma can still throw before the later updateMany fallback runs. The later updateMany block also duplicates the counter increments when profiles do exist.
open_findings.impact: Follow creation can still fail for users without Profile rows, and successful follows can inflate profile counters by two instead of one.

proof_hooks.summary: The transaction uses tx.profile.update for follower and following profiles, which requires both Profile rows to exist.
A second updateMany block increments the same counters again after the transaction, so the attempted fallback creates counter drift.

suggested_fix.approach: Review the existing case file and apply the documented remediation.
tests_needed: Re-run the relevant LYRA audit agent.

open_findings.title: Unsafe property access in message sender mapping (messaging.service.ts:500)

history.notes: Status synced from Linear (PLP-102): In Review -> fixed_pending_verify
Verified by source review: sender is included in the Prisma result and the schema relation is required.
Status changed by synthesizer synthesized-20260427-223921

open_findings.description: The message sender mapping is backed by a Prisma create query that includes sender.profile, and the schema requires Message.senderId with a required sender relation. With that include shape, sender is materialized before the mapping reads sender.id, sender.username, and sender.profile.
open_findings.impact: The reviewed sendMessage mapping has the sender relation included before response shaping, so the prior missing-include runtime access issue is resolved for this code path.

proof_hooks.summary: message.create includes sender with nested profile before messageWithSender is built.
Prisma schema declares Message.senderId as required and Message.sender as a required User relation with onDelete Cascade.

suggested_fix.approach: Review the existing case file and apply the documented remediation.
tests_needed: Re-run the relevant LYRA audit agent.

open_findings.title: Unhandled promise rejection in useMessaging hook error callback

history.notes: Status synced from Linear (PLP-103): In Review -> fixed_pending_verify
Verification failed: error callbacks are still invoked without guarding returned promises.
Status changed by synthesizer synthesized-20260427-223921

open_findings.description: useMessaging still invokes onError and onErrorRef.current directly from socket handlers and catch blocks. Because async functions are assignable to a void callback type in React/TypeScript, a rejecting async onError callback can still produce an unhandled promise rejection.
open_findings.impact: Consumer-provided async error handlers can still reject outside the hook control flow, causing noisy unhandled rejections and potentially bypassing user-facing error recovery.

proof_hooks.summary: UseMessagingOptions declares onError as a void callback, but async callbacks can still be passed by consumers.
Socket error handlers and catch blocks call onError/onErrorRef.current without Promise.resolve(...).catch handling.

suggested_fix.approach: Review the existing case file and apply the documented remediation.
tests_needed: Re-run the relevant LYRA audit agent.

Verification failed: remaining avatar/thumbnail images still use empty alt text in user-facing contexts.
Status changed by synthesizer synthesized-20260427-223921

open_findings.description: Several user/avatar or listing thumbnail images still use empty alt text, including DM inbox search results, attendee avatars, member avatars, and order listing thumbnails. Some decorative thumbnails may be acceptable, but interactive user/avatar images still need meaningful labels or an accessible text alternative tied to the control.
open_findings.impact: Screen reader users can encounter unlabeled visual identity cues in people-picking, attendee, member, and order contexts.

proof_hooks.summary: DMInbox search-result avatar renders alt="" inside an interactive user selection button.
AttendeeList renders attendee avatar images with alt="".
OrderCard renders the listing thumbnail with alt="".

suggested_fix.approach: Review the existing case file and apply the documented remediation.

open_findings.title: Tool endpoints use one shared workspace for all authenticated users

suggested_fix.approach: Derive workspace_root from user_id under a base path via WorkspacePolicy.workspace_root_for(user_id)
tests_needed: Two users with different JWTs cannot read each other's workspace files

history.notes: Imported from LYRA audit suite finding f-a50b7176 (PLP-562).
Implemented per-user workspace isolation via WorkspacePolicy. All 20 isolation tests pass.
Verified by source review: all tool file operations are scoped by authenticated user_id.
Status changed by synthesizer synthesized-20260427-223921

open_findings.description: Tool endpoints now derive workspace paths from the authenticated JWT subject. WorkspacePolicy creates base_workspace_dir/user_id sandboxes, ToolService requires user_id on public file operations, and routes pass current_user.user_id from the auth dependency.
open_findings.impact: Authenticated users no longer share one filesystem workspace through these tool endpoints; traversal checks also prevent paths from escaping the per-user sandbox.

proof_hooks.summary: WorkspacePolicy.workspace_root_for(user_id) creates and validates a per-user root under TOOL_WORKSPACE_BASE.
ToolService public methods require user_id and resolve paths through WorkspacePolicy.safe_path.
Tool routes require CurrentUser and pass current_user.user_id into ToolService for list/read/write/delete operations.
get_current_user validates Bearer JWT and rejects missing sub before constructing AuthenticatedUser.

open_findings.title: Follow creation increments profile counters twice
open_findings.description: FollowsService.followUser increments follower/following profile counters inside the transaction, then immediately performs a second updateMany increment after the transaction. A successful follow can therefore add 2 to each denormalized counter for a single Follow row.
open_findings.impact: Follower/following counts drift upward and user profiles show inflated social proof; any ranking or recommendations based on these counters become unreliable.

proof_hooks.summary: Transaction increments both profile counters before returning the created Follow row.
A second updateMany block increments the same follower/following counters again.

suggested_fix.approach: Remove the post-transaction updateMany block or replace the transaction updates with updateMany, but keep exactly one counter increment per successful Follow create.
tests_needed: Add or run a follow/unfollow service test that asserts counters change by exactly one.

history.notes: Created during LYRA audit pass.

open_findings.title: Music phase-2 vertical is exposed in production UI without a feature flag
open_findings.description: The project expectations state that the Music phase-2 vertical must not be accessible or visible in production UI until backend models are complete. The web app includes public /music routes, an onboarding link to /music, and Music copy in product pages without an observed production feature flag gate.
open_findings.impact: Production users can enter an incomplete vertical, creating broken or misleading product flows and violating a critical launch constraint.

proof_hooks.summary: /music renders MusicDiscoveryPage and exposes upload and artist-profile CTAs.
Onboarding includes a Music step that links to /music.
Expectation 8.1 forbids exposing Music phase-2 in production without complete backend support.

suggested_fix.approach: Gate all Music routes and navigation entries behind a production-safe feature flag until the vertical is explicitly ready, or document backend completeness and update the expectation.
tests_needed: Build web with the flag disabled and verify /music is not reachable from navigation/onboarding.

history.notes: Created during LYRA audit pass.

open_findings.title: CI and deploy web builds omit required NEXT_PUBLIC_WS_URL
open_findings.description: apps/web/next.config.js now throws when NEXT_PUBLIC_WS_URL is missing, but the CI Build Web step only sets NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL and the deploy Build Web step sets neither public URL. In a clean GitHub Actions environment without apps/web/.env.local, next build will fail before deployment artifacts are produced.
open_findings.impact: Main-branch deploys can be blocked by environment validation even when application code compiles locally with a checked-in or local .env file.

proof_hooks.summary: next.config.js requires NEXT_PUBLIC_WS_URL during build/start.
CI Build Web exports NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL but not NEXT_PUBLIC_WS_URL.
Deploy Build Web runs npm --prefix apps/web run build without NEXT_PUBLIC_* env.

suggested_fix.approach: Provide NEXT_PUBLIC_WS_URL and NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL in CI/deploy build environments, preferably via repository/environment variables or explicit workflow env entries.
tests_needed: Run next build in a clean env with only the workflow-provided variables.

history.notes: Created during LYRA audit pass.
